arts.ttl: Baldanzi leaves Italy under-21 camp, returns to Roma
arts.sum: Tommaso Baldanzi has returned to Roma’s training ground, Trigoria, after leaving the Italy Under-21 national team camp. The young midfielder will not participate in the upcoming friendly match against Ukraine scheduled for Tuesday, November 19th at 18:15. The Italian Football Federation (FIGC) released an official statement citing Baldanzi’s “unavailability” for the Ukraine match as the reason for his departure. However, it’s important to note that there are no reports of any injury concerns for the player. Baldanzi had already missed the previous friendly match against France on November 15th, which ended in a 2-2 draw. arts.ttl: “With Italy, I made just one mistake, against Switzerland,” says Spalletti
arts.sum: Italy’s national team coach, Luciano Spalletti, recently held a press conference to discuss the upcoming Nations League match against France. The Azzurri need to avoid a loss by more than two goals to secure first place in their group. Spalletti expressed his gratitude towards Inter Milan for their support, stating, “First of all, I thank Inter for providing us with excellent facilities. They also made available the Carboni team for practice. I felt well taken care of here.” Reflecting on the legendary Gigi Riva, Spalletti remarked, “He was a man of action. arts.ttl: UEFA Nations League Preview: Italy vs France – Team News, Line-ups & Prediction
arts.sum: Sunday is the day of reckoning in the UEFA Nations League top flight as Italy and France go head-to-head in Group A2's top-table six-pointer at Stadio San Siro. With both teams securing a berth in the quarter-finals with a game to spare, the stakes are significantly lower than when they met in the iconic 2006 FIFA World Cup final. arts.ttl: Digne: France out for revenge against Italy
arts.sum: France will be out to avenge their defeat from earlier this year when they take on Italy in a UEFA Nations League clash at the San Siro in Milan on Sunday, defender Lucas Digne said.
